system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;

}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;

}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1.0;
    }

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(

blocks
(

    hex (8 11 10 9 12 14 13 15) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(

        (
            (0 1 9 8)

        faces
        (
            (15 14 13 12)

    }

        (
            (3 11 10 2)
        );
    }

    frontAndBack

        faces
        (
            (0 1 2 3)

    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

    {
        type            fixedValue;

    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      p;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

dimensions      [1 -1 -2 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 1;

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    bottom
    {
        type            symmetryPlane;
    }

    top
    {
        type            waveTransmissive;
        field           p;
        psi             thermo:psi;
        gamma           1.4;
        fieldInf        1;
        lInf            1;
        value           uniform 1;
    }

    wedge
    {
        type            slip;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 8 9 1) 4(3 2 10 11) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/experiment-finetuned-allrun_commands/single_knockout/knockout_allrun_commands/runs/wedge/2/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 39.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Inward-pointing face normals

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9) (60 30 1) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(0 8 9 1) 4(3 2 10 11)
```

This is the same error as Attempt 1, meaning my previous fix didn't work. Let me carefully analyze the vertex coordinates and block topology.

Looking at the vertices more carefully:

**Vertices:**
- 0: (-0.15242, 0, -0.005)
- 1: (0.3048, 0, -0.005)
- 2: (0.3048, 0.09144, -0.005)
- 3: (-0.15242, 0.09144, -0.005)

These form a rectangle in the xy-plane at z=-0.005. When looking from **+z toward the origin**, the counter-clockwise order would be: 0 → 3 → 2 → 1. But the block uses `hex (0 3 2 1 ...)`, which goes 0 → 3 → 2 → 1. That's actually **clockwise** when viewed from +z, giving a downward-pointing normal.

However, the error message shows the problematic faces as `4(0 8 9 1)` and `4(3 2 10 11)`. These correspond to the **top/bottom faces** of the blocks (since edges 4 and 3 are the vertical edges connecting the two layers in z). So the issue isn't just the bottom face — it's also the top face orientation.

Let me trace through Block 0: `hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9)`

- Bottom face: vertices 0, 3, 2, 1 → This is the face at z=-0.005. Order 0→3→2→1 is clockwise when viewed from +z → **inward normal**.
- Top face: vertices 8, 11, 10, 9 → This is the face at z=+0.005. Order 8→11→10→9 is also clockwise when viewed from +z → **inward normal**.

Both horizontal faces have inward-pointing normals. I need to flip the vertex ordering so that the **first three vertices** of each face follow the right-hand rule with the normal pointing **outward** (i.e., away from the interior of the block).

For a hex block, if we want the normal of the face defined by vertices (i,j,k,l) to point in the +y direction (which would be outward for the bottom/top faces in this geometry), the vertex ordering should be such that when walking from i→j→k, the thumb points in the desired normal direction.

But perhaps a simpler approach: **reverse the entire vertex sequence** for both blocks. Instead of defining them as `hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9)`, define them as `hex (1 2 3 0 9 10 11 8)`. This reverses the winding direction, making the bottom face go 1→2→3→0, which is counter-clockwise when viewed from +z → normal pointing **upward** (+z direction), which is outward for the bottom face.

Wait — let me reconsider. Actually, looking at the coordinate system:
- The wedge occupies the region where y ≥ x·tan(15°) ≈ 0.2679x. Since tan(15°)≈0.2679, and 0.09144/0.3048≈0.3 > 0.2679, the wedge angle is indeed ~15°. 
- The bottom face at z=-0.005 spans from x=-0.15242 to 0.3048, y=0 to 0.09144. The wedge surface is one of the side faces, not the bottom.

Actually, let me visualize this differently. The key insight is that **both blocks share the same geometric shape** — they're just stacked vertically. The problem is that the vertex ordering makes the bottom face have an inward normal.

A reliable fix: **swap vertices 1↔3 and 9↔11** in both block definitions. This flips the winding direction of the bottom/top faces without fundamentally changing the block shape.

So change:
- Block 0: `hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9)` → `hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11)`
- Block 1: `hex (8 11 10 9 12 14 13 15)` → `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)`

Now the bottom face of Block 0 becomes vertices 0,1,2,3. Viewed from +z, 0→1→2→3 is counter-clockwise → normal pointing **upward** (+z), which is outward. Perfect.

### Error 2: `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Cannot find mesh

This is a cascading error — `blockMesh` failed, so no mesh exists for the solver. Once the mesh is fixed, this will resolve automatically.

## Summary

**Primary Fix**: Reverse the vertex ordering in both block definitions so that the bottom/top faces have outward-pointing normals. Specifically:
- Block 0: Change `hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9)` to `hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11)`
- Block 1: Change `hex (8 11 10 9 12 14 13 15)` to `hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)`

This ensures the bottom face (at z=-0.005) has vertices ordered 0→1→2→3, which is counter-clockwise when viewed from +z, giving an upward-pointing normal (outward from the block interior). The top face similarly gets the correct orientation.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change Block 0 definition from hex (0 3 2 1 8 11 10 9) to hex (0 1 2 3 8 9 10 11); Change Block 1 definition from hex (8 11 10 9 12 14 13 15) to hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
